title: NekoServe Service-Flow Simulation Report
methodologyBody: This report was produced by NekoServe. The simulation uses discrete-event simulation (SimPy): customer inter-arrival times are exponential (a Poisson process), and service times are normal (standard deviation 0.2 of the mean, floored at 1 minute). The queueing-theory comparison uses Kingman's M/M/c wait-time approximation, alongside a reneging-adjusted rho correction (Dbeis & Al-Sahili, 2024).
footer: Generated by the NekoServe service-flow simulation platform
